companyInfo.name: Sundarban Greenland Tourism
companyInfo.tagline: Discover the Mystique of the Mangroves
companyInfo.address: 248, Fulbagan Road, Gr. Floor, Shop No-5, P.O.-Baghajatin, P.S.-Patuli, Kolkata 700086, West Bengal, India.

packages.title: 1 Day Sundarban Explorer
packages.description: A power-packed single-day expedition into the wild Sundarbans. Start from Kolkata early morning, cruise through the world's largest mangrove delta, visit 3 iconic watchtowers, and return by evening – all with breakfast and lunch served fresh on the boat.

itinerary.activity: Departure from Kolkata. Three-hour scenic drive to Sundarban Godkhali with a tea break on the way.
Board the boat at Godkhali. Sundarban adventure begins! Head toward the mangrove creeks and delta region.
Sajnekhali Tiger Reserve – visit the watchtower, wildlife museum & crocodile enclosure.
Sudhanyakhali Watchtower – prime area for spotting deer, crocodiles, wild boar, monitor lizards, exotic birds & the Royal Bengal Tiger.
Dobanki Tiger Reserve – walk the famous 896-meter canopy walk through the treetops.
Breakfast & lunch served fresh on the boat – authentic Bengali cuisine.
Sundarban jungle trip concludes. Head back to Kolkata by AC vehicle.
Arrive back in Kolkata.

inclusions: Transportation from Kolkata by AC vehicle
Mechanised boat with beds & European toilet
exclusions: Non-Indian guests pay separate Tiger Reserve permit fees
thingsToCarry: Specific medicines for cough, cold, stomach upset etc.
Light baggage that is easy to carry
Carry cash – ATM access is limited (only SBI ATM at Gosaba)
importantNotes: Itinerary may change based on weather & tide conditions. All destinations will be covered.
Food menu may change based on availability.
Vehicle or boat may be changed for safety reasons without prior notice.

packages.title: Sundarban 1 Night 2 Days Adventure
packages.description: Our most popular short getaway! Depart from Kolkata by AC vehicle, spend a night at an eco-resort nestled beside the Sundarban Tiger Reserve, and enjoy a full-day thrilling boat safari through the mangrove creeks with folk dance, campfire, and authentic Bengali cuisine.

itinerary.activity: Pick up from Indian Museum (Park Street Metro) at 8:00 AM. Second pickup from P.C. Chandra Garden, Science City at 8:30 AM. Depart by AC vehicle.
Three-and-a-half hour scenic drive to Godkhali with breakfast & tea break on the way.
Arrive at the hotel, located opposite the Sundarban Tiger Reserve – surrounded by village, forests & river breeze.
Lunch – authentic Sundarban cuisine at the hotel.
Short boat ride to the Sundarban Bird Jungle with a mesmerizing sunset view. Return to hotel by 6 PM.
Local folk dance performance with complimentary snacks, tea & coffee.
Campfire & music (winter season only).
Dinner & overnight stay at hotel.
Depart for Sundarban delta safari by boat.
Begin jungle safari at Sajnekhali Tiger Reserve with government-certified tourist guide.

Thrilling boat safari through rivers, islands & narrow creeks – Pirkhali, Sarakkhali, Gazikhali, Deul Varani, Banbibi Varani. Visit Sudhanyakhali Watchtower – spot deer, crocodiles, wild boar, snakes, monitor lizards, birds & the Royal Bengal Tiger.
Dobanki Tiger Reserve – the famous 896-meter canopy walk through a netted treetop enclosure.
Visit Panchamukhni – the stunning 5-river junction.
Safari concludes. Head back to Kolkata by AC vehicle.
Arrive back in Kolkata.

inclusions: Transportation from Kolkata by AC vehicle (sharing basis)
Mechanised boat with beds & European toilet
All meals – Breakfast, Lunch, Evening Tea & Snacks, Dinner, Bed Tea
exclusions: Non-Indian guests pay separate Tiger Reserve permit fees
thingsToCarry: Specific medicines for cough, cold, stomach upset etc.
Light baggage that is easy to carry
Carry cash – ATM access is limited (only SBI ATM at Gosaba)

packages.title: Standard 2 Nights 3 Days Sundarban Tour
packages.description: An ideal budget-friendly Sundarban tour covering all the major attractions over 3 days. Pick up from Canning Railway Station, enjoy wildlife safaris through multiple tiger reserve areas, visit historic bungalows, and experience authentic village life – all at a pocket-friendly price.

itinerary.activity: Pick up from Canning Railway Station by auto/Tata Magic. Head to Godkhali Launch Ghat to board the boat.
Lunch at the resort.
Arrive at Pakhirala – located opposite the Sundarban Tiger Reserve, surrounded by village, forests & river breeze.
Short boat ride into the Sundarban Bird Jungle with stunning sunset views. Return by 6 PM.
Local folk dance with complimentary snacks, tea & coffee.
Dinner & overnight stay at hotel.
Morning departure for Sundarban delta safari by boat.
Jungle safari at Sajnekhali Tiger Reserve with government tourist guide.

Boat safari through rivers, islands & creeks – Pirkhali, Banbibi Varani, Sundarkhali, Khonakhali, Choragaji, Deualvarani, Sarakkhali, Gazikhali, Nobanki. Visit Sudhanyakhali Watchtower for tiger territory wildlife viewing.
Dobanki Tiger Reserve – 896-meter canopy walk through the treetop enclosure.
Visit Panchamukhni 5-river junction & Jharkhali Tiger Reserve watchtower by boat.
Return to Pakhirala after the thrilling safari.
Dinner at hotel.
Breakfast, then local village walk – a truly fantastic experience. Visit Hamilton Bungalow & Rabindranath Tagore Bungalow.
Transfer by boat to reach the car. Lunch provided on the way.
Arrive at Canning / Kolkata.

packages.title: Honeymoon Special – 1 Night 2 Days
highlights: Premium Hotel – Larica Inn / Sonar Bangla
packages.description: Celebrate your love amidst the mystic mangroves! Our exclusive honeymoon package features a stay at premium properties like Larica Inn Sundarban or Hotel Sonar Bangla Sundarban, private boat safari, romantic candlelight dinner, and a mesmerizing sunset cruise through the creeks.

itinerary.activity: Pick up from Indian Museum (Park Street Metro) or Science City. Depart by private AC vehicle.
Arrive at premium hotel – Larica Inn Sundarban or Hotel Sonar Bangla Sundarban. Welcome drink & check-in.
Special couple's lunch – fresh Bengali cuisine at the hotel restaurant.
Romantic sunset cruise through the Sundarban Bird Jungle. Enjoy the magical golden hour over the river.
Folk dance cultural programme with complimentary snacks & beverages.
Romantic candlelight dinner & overnight stay at premium hotel.
Breakfast at hotel. Depart for Sundarban boat safari.
Sajnekhali Tiger Reserve visit with government tourist guide.
Sudhanyakhali Watchtower – explore tiger territory & spot wildlife.
Dobanki Tiger Reserve – 896-meter canopy walk through the treetops.
Panchamukhni 5-river junction. Lunch on the boat.
Safari concludes. Return journey to Kolkata by AC vehicle.
Arrive back in Kolkata.

packages.title: Sundarban Hilsa Festival 2026 | Ilish Utsav
packages.description: The Sundarban Hilsa Festival 2026 offers tourists and locals a wide range of activities and events. From cooking demonstrations and food stalls to cultural performances and fishing expeditions, experience the deep-rooted connection between local communities and the Hilsa fish.

itinerary.activity: Pick up and arrival. Packed Breakfast with Mineral Water.
Lunch at Resort: Basmati Rice, Green Salad, Dal, Seasonal Veg Curry, Ilish Paturi (2 Pcs), Chutney and Papad.
Snacks: Veg Pakora / Egg Devil with Tea/Coffee.
Rice / Roti, Salad, Dal, Veg Curry, Chicken Curry, Sweets, and Hilsa Dishes.
Luchi, Dal/ Aloor Dam, Sandwich, Boiled Egg / Omelette, Fruits, Sweets, Tea/Coffee.
Lunch (On Board): Steamed Basmati Rice, Green Salad, Mung Dal, Begun Bhaja, Ichor/Potol Chingri, Doi Ilish (2 Pcs), Chatni, Papad, and Mishti Doi.
Snacks: Chicken Pakora / Paneer Pakora, Tea/Coffee.
Chapati/ Veg. Fried Rice, Green Salad, Veg Manchurian, Chilli Paneer/ Chilli Chicken (Pre-Plated), Sweets, Hilsa Fry.
Kachuri- Chana Masala, Bread Toast, Butter, Jam, Fruits, Sweets, Tea/Coffee.
Farewell Lunch (On Board): Green Salad, Polao, Bhaja Ilish, Chicken Kasha, Chatni and Papad.

inclusions: Boat Safari and Forest Entry Fees
importantNotes: Wildlife sightings are subject to chance.
No plastics allowed in the reserve.

foods.name: Sundarban Mud Crab
foods.description: Jumbo-sized mud crabs harvested from the mangrove estuaries, prepared in a rich, spicy mustard gravy – a Sundarban signature dish that is unforgettable.

foods.name: Vetki Fish Fry
foods.description: The prized Barramundi (Vetki) fish, lightly marinated with turmeric and chili, then pan-fried to golden perfection. A staple on every Sundarban boat trip.

foods.name: Ilish (Hilsa) Bhapa
foods.description: The king of Bengali fish, steamed in banana leaves with mustard paste, coconut, and green chilies. Available seasonally during the monsoon months.

foods.name: Sundarban Mutton Curry
foods.description: Slow-cooked country mutton in a rich onion-tomato gravy with hand-ground spices, served with steaming rice – the ultimate comfort food after a day of safari.

testimonials.name: Anirban Chatterjee
testimonials.text: An absolutely magical experience! The boat safari through the mangroves at sunrise was breathtaking. Our guide knew exactly where to spot the wildlife. We even saw fresh tiger pugmarks! The food on the boat was incredible – the Hilsa preparation was the best I've ever had.

testimonials.name: Priya Sharma
testimonials.text: We chose the 2 Nights/3 Days package for our family vacation and it exceeded every expectation. The eco-resort was comfortable, the food was authentic Bengali, and the Dobanki canopy walk was the highlight for our kids. Truly a world-class experience!

testimonials.name: Rajesh Kumar
testimonials.text: The 1 Night 2 Days package was perfect for a weekend getaway. Pick up from Science City was super convenient. The folk dance and campfire at night were memorable. Sudhanyakhali watchtower views were stunning – we spotted deer and a monitor lizard!

testimonials.name: Sneha Banerjee
testimonials.text: Booked the Honeymoon Special at Larica Inn and it was absolutely dreamy! The candlelight dinner by the river, the private boat safari, and the sunset cruise – everything was perfectly arranged. Best honeymoon decision ever!

testimonials.name: Amit Dasgupta
testimonials.text: Being a solo traveler, I was a bit nervous, but the team made me feel completely at home. The 1 Day Trip was perfectly organized – from the morning pickup to the evening drop. The Sajnekhali watchtower views were stunning. Great value for money!

destinations.title: The Royal Bengal Tiger of Sundarbans
destinations.excerpt: Home to over 100 Royal Bengal Tigers, the Sundarbans is one of the last strongholds of this magnificent predator.
destinations.content: The Sundarbans is the only mangrove forest in the world where the Royal Bengal Tiger has adapted to a semi-aquatic lifestyle. These magnificent creatures swim between islands, hunt fish and crabs, and have developed unique behavioral patterns found nowhere else on Earth. The Sundarban Tiger Reserve, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, protects approximately 100+ tigers in its core area. Unlike their counterparts in mainland forests, Sundarban tigers are exceptional swimmers and have been known to cover distances of up to 8 kilometers across tidal waterways. The best chances of spotting a tiger are from the watchtowers at Sudhanyakhali, Sajnekhali, and Netidhopani, especially during the winter months of November to February when tigers come to the creek banks to bask in the sun.

destinations.title: The Living Mangrove Forests
destinations.excerpt: The world's largest mangrove ecosystem spanning 10,000 sq km across India and Bangladesh.
destinations.content: The Sundarbans mangrove forest is one of the largest tidal halophytic mangrove forests in the world, spanning approximately 10,000 square kilometers across India and Bangladesh. Designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1987, this remarkable ecosystem is home to 64 plant species, including the iconic Sundari trees that give the forest its name. The mangroves serve as a critical buffer against cyclones and tsunamis, protecting millions of people living along the coast. The intricate root systems of the mangroves create a unique underwater habitat that supports an incredible diversity of marine life, including the endangered Ganges River Dolphin, Irrawaddy Dolphins, and various species of sharks and rays. Walking through the mangrove forests on elevated boardwalks like the Dobanki Canopy Walk offers visitors a unique perspective of this living, breathing ecosystem.

destinations.title: Watchtowers & Nature Trails
destinations.excerpt: Explore the famous watchtowers of Sajnekhali, Sudhanyakhali, Dobanki, and Netidhopani.
destinations.content: The Sundarban Tiger Reserve features several strategically located watchtowers that offer visitors the best vantage points for wildlife observation. Sajnekhali Watchtower, the most accessible, is often the first stop on any Sundarban tour. It houses a small museum, a crocodile breeding center, and offers panoramic views of the surrounding mangroves. Sudhanyakhali Watchtower is considered the prime tiger-spotting location, with a freshwater pond that attracts tigers, deer, and wild boar, especially during the dry season. The Dobanki Watchtower is unique for its 500-meter canopy walk – an elevated boardwalk through the treetops that provides an aerial view of the mangrove forest. Netidhopani features the ruins of a 400-year-old Shiva temple and is steeped in local legend about the forest goddess Bon Bibi. Each watchtower provides a different perspective of the Sundarbans ecosystem.

destinations.title: Birds of the Sundarbans
destinations.excerpt: A birdwatcher's paradise with over 260 species including Kingfishers, Sea Eagles, and rare migratory visitors.
destinations.content: The Sundarbans is one of the premier birdwatching destinations in India, hosting over 260 species of birds across its diverse wetland habitats. The Sajnekhali Bird Sanctuary, nestled within the tiger reserve, is the crown jewel for ornithologists. Here, you can spot the magnificent White-bellied Sea Eagle soaring above the canopy, the brilliant Common Kingfisher diving for fish in the creeks, and large flocks of egrets and herons wading through the shallow mudflats. During the winter migration season (November to February), the Sundarbans welcomes thousands of migratory birds from Siberia, Central Asia, and Southeast Asia – including species like the Northern Pintail, Eurasian Wigeon, and the rare Spoon-billed Sandpiper. The Sundarbans is also home to several species of owls, woodpeckers, and the striking Black-capped Kingfisher. The early morning boat cruises through narrow creeks offer the best birdwatching opportunities, when the forest comes alive with the calls and songs of its feathered residents. Our expert naturalist guides carry spotting scopes and field guides to help you identify every species you encounter.

destinations.title: River Life & Local Communities
destinations.excerpt: Discover the resilient communities that call the Sundarban delta home – fishermen, honey collectors, and crab farmers.
destinations.content: The Sundarbans is not just a wilderness – it is home to over 4.5 million people who have adapted to life in one of the most challenging environments on Earth. The local communities, known as Moulis (honey collectors), Jele (fishermen), and Bawaleys (wood cutters), have developed extraordinary survival skills honed over centuries. The honey collectors venture deep into tiger territory during the spring months to harvest wild honey from giant rock bee hives – a tradition celebrated in the legend of Bon Bibi, the forest goddess who is believed to protect them from tigers. Mud crab farming has emerged as a sustainable livelihood, with families nurturing crabs in small ponds carved into the riverbanks. The fishermen of the delta navigate the treacherous tidal channels in small wooden boats, casting nets for Hilsa, Vetki, and Prawns that form the backbone of Bengal's culinary tradition. Village visits during our tours offer an authentic glimpse into this remarkable way of life – you can watch traditional net-making, learn about sustainable fishing practices, and taste freshly prepared local cuisine. Our tourism model ensures that a significant portion of tour revenue goes directly to these communities, supporting schools, healthcare, and conservation efforts.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Sundarbans?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit Sundarbans is from October to March (winter season). The weather is pleasant, wildlife is more active, and visibility is excellent. The monsoon season (July-September) is ideal for the Hilsa Festival but travel can be challenging due to heavy rains.

faqs.question: How do I reach the Sundarbans from Kolkata?
faqs.answer: From Kolkata, we pick you up from Indian Museum (Park Street Metro) or P.C. Chandra Garden (Science City). The drive to Godkhali Jetty takes approximately 3-4 hours by AC vehicle. For budget packages, pickup is from Canning Railway Station.

faqs.question: Is it safe to visit the Sundarbans?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! All our tours are conducted with expert naturalist guides, forest department permissions, and strict safety protocols. Life jackets are mandatory on all boat rides. Our guides are trained in wildlife safety and first aid.

faqs.question: Will I see a Royal Bengal Tiger?
faqs.answer: Tiger sightings are never guaranteed as these are wild animals in their natural habitat. However, our experienced guides know the best spots and times for sightings. On 2-3 day packages, the chances improve significantly. Even without a direct sighting, you'll likely see pugmarks, scratch marks, and other signs of tiger activity.

faqs.question: What should I carry for a Sundarban trip?
faqs.answer: Essential items include: comfortable cotton clothes, sunscreen, insect repellent, binoculars, camera with zoom lens, prescribed medications, a light jacket (for winter months), waterproof bags for electronics, valid photo ID, and cash (only SBI ATM available at Gosaba).

faqs.question: Are the tours suitable for children and elderly?
faqs.answer: Yes! Our boat safaris are comfortable and suitable for all ages. Children aged 1-4 travel free, and those aged 4-8 get 50% off. We recommend the 1-Day or 1-Night packages for very young children or elderly travelers.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation and payment policy?
faqs.answer: We require 50% advance payment at the time of booking. The remaining 50% is payable before the trip. Cancellations made 7+ days before departure receive a full refund (minus processing fees). Cancellations within 3-7 days receive a 50% refund. Cancellations within 72 hours are non-refundable.

faqs.question: Do you provide vegetarian food?
faqs.answer: Yes, we cater to all dietary requirements including vegetarian, vegan, and Jain food. You can select your meal preference (Veg/Non-Veg) at the time of booking so we can make arrangements. Our chefs prepare fresh, hygienic meals using local ingredients.
